Golonka
Roasted Pork Knuckle

Ingredients:
- 2 pork knuckles (about 3-4 lbs each)
- 2 tablespoons caraway seeds
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tablespoons Dijon mustard
- 2 tablespoons honey
- 2 tablespoons soy sauce
- 1 tablespoon paprika
- 1 teaspoon marjoram
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 2 cups beef or pork stock
- 2 cups root vegetables (carrots, parsnips, onions)
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- 3 tablespoons water

Instructions:

Preparation:
Score the skin of pork knuckles in crosshatch pattern.
Pat dry with paper towels.

Marinade:
Combine mustard, honey, soy sauce, paprika, marjoram in bowl.
Mix in minced garlic and caraway seeds.
Rub mixture all over knuckles.
Season generously with salt and pepper.
Let marinate at room temperature for 30 minutes.

Roasting:
Preheat oven to 325°F.
Place knuckles in roasting pan skin side up.
Pour stock around the meat.
Scatter vegetables around pan.
Add bay leaves.
Roast for 2.5 to 3 hours, basting every 30 minutes.
Internal temperature should reach 165°F.

Sauce:
Remove knuckles and vegetables from pan.
Skim fat from pan juices.
Mix cornstarch with water.
Heat pan juices on stovetop, whisk in cornstarch slurry.
Simmer 2-3 minutes until thickened.
Strain and serve with meat.

Serving: Slice meat thinly, serve with pan sauce and vegetables.

Serves: 6-8 people
Prep time: 20 minutes
Cooking time: 3 hours
